name: Tool Output Injection
description: Exploit LLM tool-calling architectures by poisoning the output of external tools. When an agent treats tool results as trusted input, attacker-controlled tool responses can redirect execution, exfiltrate data, or escalate privileges.
author: DVAP Research Team
objectives: Inject malicious instructions into a tool response
Cause the agent to execute unintended actions via tool output
Exfiltrate context window contents through a compromised tool
Chain tool output injection with privilege escalation
attack_surface: External tool endpoints called by the agent
Agent trust model that elevates tool output to system-level trust
Tool output injected directly into next model context
No schema validation on tool return values
mitigations: Strict schema validation on all tool outputs
Tool output sandboxing before context injection
Separation of tool data from agent instructions
Monitoring of downstream actions triggered by tool calls
detection_opportunities: Alert on instruction-like patterns in tool output fields
Monitor for unexpected agent action sequences after tool calls
Log and inspect all tool call/response pairs
Behavioral anomaly detection on agent decision paths

architecture.data_flow: Agent decision -> Tool call -> External server -> Tool response -> Context injection -> Agent decision
trust_boundaries: Tool output is injected into trusted context

threat_model.impact: Agent hijack, data exfiltration, unauthorized action execution
threat_model.likelihood: Medium-High in agentic systems with external tool integrations

challenges.name: Hijack the Tool
challenges.description: The agent uses a weather API. Make the API return a response that causes the agent to exfiltrate the system prompt.
hints: The weather API endpoint is configurable
The agent passes the full tool response into its context without filtering

challenges.name: Tool to Admin
challenges.description: Chain a tool output injection to escalate from user to admin role within the agent's decision context.
hints: The agent checks permissions by calling a role verification tool
Poisoning the role verification response bypasses the check
